title: Build an Image Gallery with lightbox
description: Build a responsive image grid that opens a full-screen lightbox.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Responsive thumbnail grid; click an image to open a full-screen lightbox.
- Lightbox supports next/prev, keyboard arrows, Esc to close, and a caption/counter.
- Bonus: thumbnails strip and pinch/zoom.

**What interviewers look for:** index state shared between grid and lightbox, keyboard handling + focus trap, preloading neighbors, responsive CSS grid.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Drag-and-drop file uploader with configurable chunk size
description: Build a droppable upload zone that splits files into configurable-size chunks.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A droppable zone (drag-over highlight) plus a file picker; accept multiple files.
- Split each file into chunks of a **configurable `chunkSize`** using `Blob.slice`, then upload chunks sequentially/with limited concurrency.
- Show per-file progress, support pause/resume and retry of failed chunks.

**What interviewers look for:** `Blob.slice` chunking math, concurrency control, progress aggregation, drag-and-drop events (`dragover`/`drop`, `dataTransfer`), resumable-upload thinking.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build an Analog Clock
description: Build an analog clock with moving hour, minute and second hands.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Render a clock face; rotate hour/minute/second hands to the current time, updating every second.
- Smooth second-hand motion; correct angle math.

**What interviewers look for:** angle calculation (degrees per unit), interval cleanup, SVG/CSS transforms, drift-free updates.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Digital Clock
description: Build a live digital clock with 12/24-hour toggle.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Display the current time updating every second; toggle between 12h (AM/PM) and 24h formats.
- Zero-pad units.

**What interviewers look for:** `setInterval` cleanup, time formatting, derived format state.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Dark Mode Toggle
description: Build a theme toggle that persists the user's choice.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Toggle between light/dark; apply a class/attribute on the root and persist to `localStorage`.
- Respect the system preference on first load (`prefers-color-scheme`).

**What interviewers look for:** persistence, no flash of wrong theme, system-preference detection, controlled toggle.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Dual Range (price) Slider
description: Build a two-handle range slider for a min–max price filter.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Two draggable handles on a track selecting a min and max; handles can't cross.
- Show the selected range, snap to `step`, and emit `[min, max]`.

**What interviewers look for:** pointer-event dragging, value↔pixel mapping, clamping handles against each other, accessibility (`aria-valuenow`).

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Tooltip
description: Build a tooltip that positions itself around a target on hover/focus.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Show a tooltip on hover/focus with a configurable placement (top/right/bottom/left).
- Flip/shift to stay within the viewport; delay show/hide.

**What interviewers look for:** positioning math from `getBoundingClientRect`, collision flipping, hover+focus accessibility, portal rendering.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Tag / Chip Input
description: Build an input that turns entries into removable tag chips.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Type + Enter (or comma) to add a chip; click ✕ or Backspace on empty input to remove.
- Prevent duplicates; emit the tag list.

**What interviewers look for:** keyboard handling (Enter/Backspace), dedup, controlled list, focus management.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Transfer List
description: Build a dual-list picker that moves items between two columns.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Two lists (available/selected); select items and move them across with buttons; move-all and search.
- Preserve order; emit the selected set.

**What interviewers look for:** selection state per side, immutable moves between lists, search filtering, disabled-button logic.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build Nested Checkboxes (tri-state)
description: Build a checkbox tree where parents reflect child selection.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A tree of checkboxes; checking a parent toggles all descendants.
- A parent shows checked / unchecked / **indeterminate** based on its children; checking children rolls up.

**What interviewers look for:** recursive tree updates, indeterminate computation, two-way propagation (down + up), `indeterminate` DOM property.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Password Generator
description: Build a password generator with length and character-set options.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Generate a random password with a configurable length and toggles for upper/lower/digits/symbols.
- Show a strength meter and a copy-to-clipboard button.

**What interviewers look for:** ensuring at least one of each selected set, randomness, clipboard API, derived strength.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Segmented Control
description: Build a segmented control (button group) with an animated indicator.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A row of options where one is active; clicking switches it with a sliding indicator.
- Controlled `value` + `onChange`; keyboard arrow navigation.

**What interviewers look for:** active-index state, measuring/animating the indicator, accessibility (rad/tablist roles).

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Breadcrumbs component
description: Build breadcrumbs that collapse the middle when too long.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Render a path of links with separators; the last item is the current (non-link) page.
- Collapse the middle into `…` when items exceed a max, expandable on click.

**What interviewers look for:** data-driven rendering, truncation logic, accessible `nav`/`aria-current`.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build an Avatar Group
description: Build a stacked avatar group with a +N overflow badge.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Overlap avatars; show only the first `max` and a `+N` bubble for the rest.
- Tooltip with the name on hover.

**What interviewers look for:** slicing + overflow count, z-index stacking, fallback initials for missing images.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Like Button
description: Build an animated like button with optimistic count.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Toggle liked/unliked with an animation; update the count optimistically.
- Bonus: revert on a simulated failed request.

**What interviewers look for:** optimistic UI, toggle + count derivation, animation, accessible pressed state.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Skeleton Loader
description: Build skeleton placeholders shown while content loads.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Render shimmering placeholder blocks matching the content layout; swap to real content when loaded.
- Reusable skeleton primitives (line, circle, rect).

**What interviewers look for:** loading-state modeling, CSS shimmer animation, layout-matching placeholders, reduced layout shift.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Lazy-loaded Image
description: Build an image that loads only when scrolled into view.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Defer loading the real `src` until the image is near the viewport; show a placeholder/blur first.
- Fade in on load; handle errors with a fallback.

**What interviewers look for:** IntersectionObserver, placeholder→real swap, load/error handling, cleanup.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Typewriter Effect
description: Build text that types and deletes in a loop.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Type out strings character by character, pause, delete, then move to the next string and loop.
- Configurable speed and a blinking caret.

**What interviewers look for:** timeout-driven state machine, cleanup, configurable timing, no overlapping timers.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Speed Dial Menu
description: Build a FAB that fans out action buttons on click.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A floating action button that expands into a set of action buttons (radial or vertical).
- Close on outside click or Escape; staggered animation.

**What interviewers look for:** open/close state, outside-click handling, staggered transitions, accessibility.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Search Bar with History
description: Build a search bar that remembers recent queries.
answer:
**Requirements**
- On submit, store the query in recent history (dedup, capped, persisted to `localStorage`).
- Show recent searches in a dropdown; click to re-run; clear history.

**What interviewers look for:** persistence, dedup + cap logic, dropdown + outside-click, keyboard navigation.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Collapsible Sidebar Menu
description: Build a sidebar with collapsible nested menu items.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A sidebar that collapses to icons-only and expands; nested groups expand/collapse.
- Highlight the active item; remember collapsed state.

**What interviewers look for:** nested menu data, per-group open state, collapsed vs expanded layout, accessibility.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Canvas Drawing Board
description: Build a freehand drawing board on canvas with undo and colors.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Draw freehand with the mouse/touch on a `<canvas>`; choose color and brush size; clear.
- Undo/redo by tracking strokes.

**What interviewers look for:** canvas 2D context, pointer events, stroke history for undo, redraw strategy.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Combobox (search + select)
description: Build an accessible combobox that filters and selects an option.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A text input that filters a dropdown of options; select with mouse or keyboard (↑/↓/Enter/Esc).
- Highlight the active option; show selected value; support async options.

**What interviewers look for:** ARIA combobox pattern, active-descendant keyboard nav, filtering, outside-click close.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Form with validation
description: Build a contact form with field-level and submit validation.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Fields (name/email/message) with on-blur and on-submit validation and inline error messages.
- Disable submit until valid; show a success state.

**What interviewers look for:** validation rules, touched/dirty tracking, controlled inputs, accessible error association (`aria-describedby`).

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Dropdown Menu
description: Build a button-triggered dropdown menu with keyboard support.
answer:
**Requirements**
- A trigger button toggles a menu of items; close on outside click, Escape, or selecting an item.
- Arrow-key navigation and focus management.

**What interviewers look for:** outside-click + Escape handling, focus trap/roving tabindex, ARIA menu roles.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Temperature Converter
description: Build a two-way Celsius–Fahrenheit converter.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Two inputs (°C and °F); editing one updates the other live.
- Handle empty/partial input gracefully; round sensibly.

**What interviewers look for:** two-way derived inputs without loops, conversion math, controlled inputs, edge cases.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*

title: Build a Notification Badge
description: Build a bell icon with an unread-count badge.
answer:
**Requirements**
- Show a count badge on an icon; cap display at `99+`; hide at zero.
- Bonus: a dropdown list that marks items read and decrements the count.

**What interviewers look for:** count capping, conditional rendering, derived unread count, accessible labeling.

*Reference solution and live playground coming soon.*
